The visitors center is modern with lots to see. The auditorium programs were great with comfortable seating. Lots to see on the grounds so plan on it taking a few hours. The staff were very helpful, answering all the questions put to them. The outdoor areas may be difficult if you are in a manual wheelchair so have a strong person as your helper.

What an absolute stunning place to visit.
To see the telescope that actually discovered Pluto was amazıng
All of the history that you have is completely aww inspiring.
And to even have a show that's on the roof rooftop with heated seats explaining about the moon an while we were there saw at least five shooting stars as the gentleman was talking.
Completely worth every penny!
The staff is all wonderful!!

Jared's  historic lecture was A+. The rest of the Observatory seemed great, but ... be careful about when you attend. At 12:30 we were charged full admission fee, but only about half the sites were open. A beautiful, edu-tainment / scientific place to visit but plan in advance to avoid disappointment! Thanks again to Jared for our lesson about (semi) planet Pluto!
